content.question: At what local cohort size do schools in peripheral Hungarian settlements become unable to form even one viable single selective-track intake class under current statutory and administrative rules, and how many settlements fall below this threshold under low-fertility scenarios?
content.reduction_path: Conduct micro-census of current selective-track provision density by settlement size and recent enrolment data; model forward using projected cohort declines and statutory class-size rules to identify settlements losing viability; distinguish geographic viability from parental demand.
